genre_refs: Emanuel Leutze 'Westward the Course of Empire Takes Its Way' (Manifest Destiny history painting)
description: A designed propaganda poster from the Department of Labor that overlays a single white serif slogan onto a reproduction of Emanuel Leutze's monumental Manifest Destiny mural 'Westward the Course of Empire Takes Its Way.' The painting depicts a crowd of 19th-century American pioneers, frontiersmen, and a Madonna-like mother-and-child cresting a rocky mountain ridge at golden sunset, one figure at the peak gesturing westward, with covered wagons and a hazy promised-land valley below. A small white DOL/eagle emblem sits centered above the bottom text. Across the lower portion, in large white serif capitals, the slogan reads 'AMERICANISM WILL PREVAIL.' The technique fuses revered Manifest Destiny / westward-expansion imagery with a present-day nationalist slogan (the tweet adds 'The Future Belongs to the Rugged Individualist'), evoking pioneer mythology, rugged individualism, and national destiny. Target audience: nationalist, traditionalist Americans, framing the administration's agenda as a continuation of America's heroic expansionist heritage.
notable_text: AMERICANISM WILL PREVAIL (caption adds: The Future Belongs to the Rugged Individualist; 'Westward the Course of Empire Takes Its Way' - Emanuel Leutze)
